cases.passage: The Indian government has been making efforts to promote innovation and entrepreneurship in the country. As part of this initiative, the Department of Industrial Policy and Promotion (DIPP) has established a system for granting patents to inventors. This system provides a framework for protecting intellectual property rights (IPRs) and encourages individuals and organizations to invest in research and development. For instance, a scientist named Rohan has developed a new type of solar panel that is more efficient and cost-effective than existing technology. He has applied for a patent for his invention and is awaiting a response from the DIPP. What type of intellectual property right is Rohan seeking to protect through his patent application?

questions.q: What is the main purpose of the DIPP's patent-granting system?
opts: (A) To restrict the use of existing technology
(B) To promote innovation and entrepreneurship
(C) To protect intellectual property rights
(D) To regulate the use of natural resources
questions.explanation: The DIPP's patent-granting system is designed to promote innovation and entrepreneurship by providing a framework for protecting intellectual property rights.

questions.q: What type of intellectual property right is Rohan seeking to protect through his patent application?
opts: (A) Copyright
(B) Trademark
(C) Patent
(D) Trade secret
questions.explanation: Rohan is seeking to protect his invention, a new type of solar panel, through a patent application.

questions.q: What is the benefit of the patent-granting system for inventors like Rohan?
opts: (A) It restricts the use of existing technology
(B) It promotes innovation and entrepreneurship
(C) It provides a framework for protecting intellectual property rights
(D) It reduces the cost of research and development
questions.explanation: The patent-granting system provides a framework for protecting intellectual property rights, which encourages inventors like Rohan to invest in research and development.

questions.q: Why is it important for Rohan to protect his intellectual property right?
opts: (A) So that others can use his invention without permission
(B) So that he can sell his invention to the highest bidder
(C) So that he can prevent others from copying his invention
(D) So that he can share his invention with the public
questions.explanation: Rohan wants to protect his intellectual property right so that he can prevent others from copying his invention and profiting from it without his permission.

questions.q: What is the role of the DIPP in the patent-granting process?
opts: (A) To grant patents to inventors
(B) To review patent applications
(C) To provide a framework for protecting intellectual property rights
(D) To regulate the use of natural resources
questions.explanation: The DIPP is responsible for reviewing patent applications and granting patents to inventors who meet the necessary criteria.

cases.passage: The music streaming service, Gaana, has been sued by the Indian music composer, Pankaj Udhas, for copyright infringement. Udhas claims that Gaana has been playing his songs without his permission and has not paid him the royalties he is entitled to. Gaana argues that it has a license to play Udhas's music from a third-party music aggregator. What type of intellectual property right is Udhas trying to protect through his lawsuit?

questions.q: What type of intellectual property right is Udhas trying to protect through his lawsuit?
opts: (A) Patent
(B) Trademark
(C) Copyright
(D) Trade secret
questions.explanation: Udhas is trying to protect his musical compositions through his lawsuit, which is a form of copyright infringement.

questions.q: Why is Udhas suing Gaana for copyright infringement?
opts: (A) Because Gaana has not paid him the royalties he is entitled to
(B) Because Gaana has played his songs without his permission
(C) Because Gaana has copied his music
(D) All of the above
questions.explanation: Udhas is suing Gaana for copyright infringement because it has played his songs without his permission and has not paid him the royalties he is entitled to.

questions.q: What is the role of the third-party music aggregator in the dispute between Udhas and Gaana?
opts: (A) To grant a license to Gaana to play Udhas's music
(B) To review Udhas's copyright claims
(C) To provide a framework for protecting intellectual property rights
(D) To regulate the use of natural resources
questions.explanation: The third-party music aggregator has granted a license to Gaana to play Udhas's music, which is a defense against Udhas's copyright infringement claims.

questions.q: Why is it important for Udhas to protect his intellectual property right?
opts: (A) So that others can use his music without permission
(B) So that he can sell his music to the highest bidder
(C) So that he can prevent others from copying his music
(D) So that he can share his music with the public
questions.explanation: Udhas wants to protect his intellectual property right so that he can prevent others from copying his music and profiting from it without his permission.

questions.q: What is the potential outcome of Udhas's lawsuit against Gaana?
opts: (A) Gaana will be forced to pay Udhas the royalties he is entitled to
(B) Gaana will be allowed to continue playing Udhas's music without permission
(C) Udhas will be awarded damages for copyright infringement
(D) The lawsuit will be dismissed
questions.explanation: If Udhas wins his lawsuit, he may be awarded damages for copyright infringement, which would compensate him for the harm caused by Gaana's actions.
